Product lines

Four products. Three zones.
Zone — Hydration
Rapid Hydration
Clean, natural flavour. No artificial sweeteners. Electrolytes and Vitamin C that hydrate nearly 3× faster than water alone.
Light workouts & everyday use
Key claims

What it does

3×
faster than water
13g
per sachet
5
flavours
  • Electrolytes + Vitamin C — replaces sweat losses, supports immune function
  • Hydrates faster than water alone via active transport mechanism
  • No artificial sweeteners — no stevia, monk fruit, or sucralose
  • Sweetened with natural cane sugar
  • Vegan. Naturally gluten-free (see note)
  • Right for: gym, hiking, short runs, hot days, travel, recovery days
  • Not for multi-hour fuelling — that's what Endurance Fuel is for
Gluten note

Naturally gluten-free but not certified. Athletes with diagnosed Celiac disease should be aware of potential trace amounts below 20ppm.

Worth knowing

The science behind faster hydration

Here's why plain water is actually a slow hydrator. To get water from your gut into your bloodstream, it has to pass through the intestinal wall passively. That takes time. When you add sodium and a small amount of sugar, the body's active transport mechanism kicks in — sodium and sugar molecules are actively pulled across the intestinal wall, and water follows them through. The result is absorption nearly 3× faster than water alone.

This is also why Rapid Hydration uses real cane sugar rather than stevia or monk fruit. Artificial sweeteners don't activate that transport mechanism — they just taste sweet. The sugar earns its place.

Mix: 1 scoop or sachet (13g) into 500–700ml water. Drink as you would water throughout the day.

Five flavours
OrangeStrawberry LemonadeLemon LimeRaspberryBerry
Zone — Fuel
Endurance Fuel
Complete energy, electrolytes, and hydration in one bottle. Simple carbs absorb directly — no GI distress. Proven as sole fuel for 24+ hour efforts.
1hr+ efforts — can be sole fuel source
The science and the argument

Why it works

200
cal per serve
610mg
sodium per serve
50g
carbs per serve

Most sports nutrition uses maltodextrin as its carb source. Maltodextrin is a chain of glucose molecules — before your muscles can use it, your gut has to break the chain apart. That digestion takes time, requires energy, and gives the digestive system more work to do mid-effort. Tailwind uses dextrose (glucose) and sucrose instead. Your small intestine absorbs them directly, no digestion step required. The energy is available to your muscles faster, and your gut is left alone.

Combining dextrose and sucrose also means two different intestinal transport pathways are working at once. Research shows this can increase the amount of carbohydrate absorbed per hour by up to 40% versus single-source glucose. That's not a marketing number — it's the reason athletes can run on Tailwind as their sole fuel for 24+ hours without gut issues.

Mix: 1–2 scoops per 500–700ml. One serve per hour. Start drinking in the first 15 minutes — don't wait until you're depleted.

Worth knowing

The sodium story

Most people think of sodium as a cramping fix. That's true, but it's the secondary benefit. The primary reason sodium is in Endurance Fuel is that it activates the glucose and fructose transport mechanism. When sodium is present in the intestine, it actively pulls both sugar molecules and water across the intestinal wall — meaning you absorb more water per sip than you would drinking plain water or a sodium-free drink.

The electrolyte profile in Endurance Fuel is designed to mirror what you actually lose in sweat: sodium, potassium, calcium, and magnesium in the right ratios. Most sports drinks deliver around 15% of hourly electrolyte needs, which is why athletes reach for salt tabs mid-race. With Tailwind, there's nothing to supplement. The bottle is the whole system.

Eight flavours
BerryLemonMandarinNakedDauwaltermelon w/ LimeGrapeRaspberry Buzz ☕Tropical Buzz ☕
Zone — Fuel (High Intensity)
High Carb Fuel
90g carbs per serve. Maximises energy uptake for high intensity racing. Requires a four-week gut training programme before use.
Gut training required
For trained athletes

How it's different from Endurance Fuel

360
cal per serve
660mg
sodium per serve
90g
carbs per serve

Endurance Fuel uses simple carbs only — absorbed fast, easy on the gut. High Carb Fuel deliberately adds maltodextrin to the mix alongside dextrose and fructose. The maltodextrin provides a more sustained energy release at very high carb loads, and the three-source blend allows the gut to absorb the full 90g per hour once it's been trained to handle it.

The tradeoff is real: this is sweeter, higher GI risk if untrained, and not suitable for someone new to Tailwind. For a criterium or time trial where you need maximum energy throughput and you've done the gut work, it's the right call. For a 6-hour trail run, Endurance Fuel is the better choice for most people.

Important

If persistent GI issues occur, Endurance Fuel is the better choice. Never push High Carb Fuel on someone who hasn't completed gut training.

Four-week gut training protocol

Building to 90g/hr

Typical gut limit without training: ~60g carbs/hour. Build progressively:

Week 1
90
minutes
60g/hr
Week 2
80
minutes
68g/hr
Week 3
70
minutes
77g/hr
Week 4
60
minutes
90g/hr ✓

Use HCF in high intensity training sessions that mimic your target event. For lighter sessions, use Rapid Hydration or Endurance Fuel. If any discomfort occurs, extend the protocol.

Zone — Recovery
Recovery Mix
Replenish, rebuild, and rehydrate in one drink. 20g complete plant-based protein, glycogen-restocking carbs, and full electrolytes. No blender needed.
Within 30 minutes of finishing
The three pillars

Replenish. Rebuild. Rehydrate.

20g
complete protein
2g
leucine per serve
3
recovery pillars

Recovery isn't one thing — it's three things happening at once. Dextrose and cane sugar restock the glycogen your muscles burned through. The protein blend repairs the muscle fibres you just tore. The electrolytes replace what you sweated out and restore fluid balance so none of the other processes are working against dehydration. Do all three together, immediately after finishing, and you recover faster than doing them separately or slowly.

The 2g of leucine per serve isn't accidental. Leucine is the specific amino acid that triggers muscle protein synthesis — the signalling cascade that tells your muscles to start rebuilding. Below a certain dose, that signal doesn't fire properly. Getting it right matters more than just hitting a total protein number.

Why fava beans?

The new formula story

The original Recovery Mix used brown rice protein plus a blend of essential amino acids to complete the protein profile. It worked well — 10g of complete protein per serve, solid recovery results. But the essential amino acid blend had a bitter flavour that was hard to mask. The formula came in chocolate, vanilla, and salted caramel, and that was the ceiling. No fruit flavours were possible.

The new formula replaces that amino acid blend with fava bean protein isolate. Fava protein has a neutral flavour profile, which means it doesn't fight back when you're trying to make a strawberry that actually tastes like strawberry. Combined with brown rice protein it still delivers a complete amino acid profile — all essential amino acids present, same as animal protein. The protein content doubled from 10g to 20g per serve. Strawberry became possible.

New formula — Fava (20g protein, recommended)
ChocolateVanillaSalted CaramelStrawberry
Original formula (10g protein — still in market)
ChocolateVanillaSalted CaramelCoffee ☕

The science — from the training materials

Why Tailwind is different.

These charts are drawn directly from the formulation data in Tailwind's product education documents. Use them when explaining Tailwind to athletes, coaches, or retail staff.

Endurance Fuel vs High Carb Fuel
Endurance Fuel200 cal / 50g carbs
High Carb Fuel360 cal / 90g carbs

Endurance Fuel is the go-to for most athletes. High Carb Fuel is for trained athletes needing maximum energy uptake in shorter, harder efforts.
Hydration speed vs plain water
Active transport mechanism — Rapid Hydration & Endurance Fuel
Tailwind (electrolytes)
~3× faster
Plain water
Baseline
Sodium activates the glucose/fructose transport mechanism in the small intestine, drawing water across the intestinal wall nearly 3× faster than passive absorption from plain water.

Simple carbs, not maltodextrin

Dextrose and sucrose absorb directly without digestion. Maltodextrin is a chain of glucose molecules that must be broken down first — taking time and energy away from working muscles.

No protein during activity

Protein is essential for recovery — but it is a documented contributor to GI distress during exercise. Tailwind keeps all activity products protein-free by design. Recovery Mix is for after.

Clean label, genuinely

Non-GMO dextrose and sucrose. Natural flavours. No artificial sweeteners, colours, or preservatives. Vegan. Gluten free. Dairy free. Soy free. Every product in the range.
